2604.20677.title: Intersectional Fairness in Large Language Models
2604.20677.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in socially sensitive settings, raising concerns about fairness and biases, particularly across intersectional demographic attributes. In this paper, we systematically evaluate intersectional fairness in six LLMs using ambiguous and disambiguated contexts from two benchmark datasets. We assess LLM behavior using bias scores, subgroup fairness metrics, accuracy, and consistency through multi-run analysis across contexts and negative and non-negative question polarities. Our results show that while modern LLMs generally perform well in ambiguous contexts, this limits the informativeness of fairness metrics due to sparse non-unknown predictions. In disambiguated contexts, LLM accuracy is influenced by stereotype alignment, with models being more accurate when the correct answer reinforces a stereotype than when it contradicts it. This pattern is especially pronounced in race-gender intersections, where directional bias toward stereotypes is stronger. Subgroup fairness metrics further indicate that, despite low observed disparity in some cases, outcome distributions remain uneven across intersectional groups. Across repeated runs, responses also vary in consistency, including stereotype-aligned responses. Overall, our findings show that apparent model competence is partly associated with stereotype-consistent cues, and no evaluated LLM achieves consistently reliable or fair behavior across intersectional settings. These findings highlight the need for evaluation beyond accuracy, emphasizing the importance of combining bias, subgroup fairness, and consistency metrics across intersectional groups, contexts, and repeated runs.
2604.20677.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被應用於社會敏感的環境中，這引發了對公平性和偏見的擔憂，特別是在交叉人口特徵方面。在本文中，我們系統地評估了六個LLMs的交叉公平性，使用來自兩個基準數據集的模糊和明確背景。我們通過多次運行分析，使用偏見分數、子群公平性指標、準確性和一致性來評估LLM的行為，涵蓋不同的背景以及正面和負面的問題極性。我們的結果顯示，儘管現代LLMs在模糊背景下的表現普遍良好，但由於稀疏的非未知預測，這限制了公平性指標的信息量。在明確背景下，LLM的準確性受到刻板印象對齊的影響，當正確答案強化刻板印象時，模型的準確性更高，而當它與刻板印象相矛盾時則較低。這一模式在種族與性別的交叉點上尤其明顯，對刻板印象的方向性偏見更強。子群公平性指標進一步表明，儘管在某些情況下觀察到的差異較小，但結果分佈在交叉群體中仍然不均勻。在重複運行中，回應的一致性也有所變化，包括與刻板印象一致的回應。總體而言，我們的研究結果顯示，明顯的模型能力在一定程度上與刻板印象一致的線索相關，且沒有一個被評估的LLM在交叉環境中達到持續可靠或公平的行為。這些發現突顯了超越準確性進行評估的必要性，強調了在交叉群體、背景和重複運行中結合偏見、子群公平性和一致性指標的重要性。
2604.20677.author: Chaima Boufaied et.al.
2604.20677.authors: Chaima Boufaied, Ronnie De Souza Santos, Ann Barcomb
